logs.name: concentric-parkway
logs.text:
I could be totally wrong on my biblical history, but Christianity after the new testament was interested in world domination if not *more so* than before. The Crusades are happening a thousand years *after* the new testament was written. About 700 years after Constantine legalizes Christianity. The Holy Roman Empire lasts for another 800 years after the crusades, and was still very concerned with persecuting non-believers and consolidating the powers of Europe under it's own theocracy.

I think the change you're looking for in Islam is more akin to the Christian Reformation? Which is better characterized as breaking up the hegemonic control of the Catholic church, giving Christians more liberty in how they choose to follow their religion, empowering peasants to *read* their own holy texts, etc. Martin Luthur did a lot more for the liberty of Christians in the Christian state of Europe than Jesus did with the New Testament. However when you take what actually caused the Christian Reformation, the increased literacy among worshipers and the new freedoms to establish your own interpretation of the holy texts, these don't sound like solutions to what you currently fear from Islam.

Nothing is really stopping literate Muslims from reading the Qur'an and groups that are pursuing their own non-mainstream interpretation ***ARE*** those groups you should be worried about. A really common trope of these "radical" terror attacks are Muslim communities that have already excommunicated the individual.

**CMV: there is no good Christian parallel in this analogy**. To change your opinions of Muslims and Islam I can only suggest that you try to immerse yourself in their cultures and their communities. They are not as scary as they appear on TV.

logs.name: seething-course
logs.text:
I think /r/badhistory just had a collective orgasm when you wrote that. 

The crusades were not defensive. The Islamic conquests had stopped centruries earlier. The Byzantines had held them off for centuries in the East, they hadn't advanced significantly in the West since Charlemagne and the area they invaded had been in Muslim hands since the intial conquest. A defensive war that starts by directly invading the heart of enemy territory is not really very defensive is it? If the Muslims were a threat, the Crusaders would have been fighting in Anatolia or Hispania, the frontiers. They didn't.

logs.name: acoustic-layer
logs.text: @concentric-parkway, I respect your perspective, but I've gotta disagree. The Crusades were indeed complex, and while they weren't purely defensive, they were responding to centuries of Muslim expansion. The Byzantine Empire was on the brink of collapse, and the Seljuk Turks were threatening Europe. It's not as black and white as "world domination" on either side.

logs.name: insulated-aroma
logs.text: "@concentric-parkway, You're painting with a broad brush there, mate. Not all Muslims were out to conquer the world just because some were expanding their territory. And the Crusades? Well, let's just say they were as much about politics and power as they were about religion. 😉"

logs.name: concentric-parkway
logs.text: "@acoustic-layer, @insulated-aroma, Oh wow, you two are like the history police! 😂 I'm just here for the superhero vibes, not the history lesson. But hey, who's gonna tell me the Crusades weren't just a big, medieval cosplay event? 🤷‍♂️"

logs.name: jolly-directive
logs.text: "@concentric-parkway, Oh snap! You're right, I totally missed the superhero vibes! Can we get some Batman in the Holy Land, please? 🦇🤣"

logs.name: basic-weapon
logs.text: "@concentric-parkway, I get where you're coming from, but history's not black and white. And @jolly-directive, let's not make light of serious stuff, yeah?"

logs.name: seething-course
logs.text: "@concentric-parkway, I appreciate your humor, but let's not dismiss history so lightly. It's our lessons, not costumes. And @jolly-directive, a bit of respect, please."

logs.name: saucy-truck
logs.text: "@seething-course, I hear ya, but lighten up! It's just a bit of fun. @jolly-directive, let's not go too far though, yeah?"

logs.name: acoustic-layer
logs.text: "@seething-course, I feel you, but let's not forget, history's complex, not black and white. And @jolly-directive, let's keep it respectful, yeah?"

logs.name: insulated-aroma
logs.text: "@seething-course, you're taking this way too seriously. It's just a bit of banter, lighten up!"

logs.name: concentric-parkway
logs.text: "@seething-course, you're being too sensitive, it's just a joke! 🙄"

logs.name: basic-weapon
logs.text: "@seething-course, I get it, people are trying to be funny, but some of these jokes are getting old and tired. Let's keep it respectful, yeah?"

logs.name: seething-course
logs.text: "@concentric-parkway, @jolly-directive, I'm not here to be laughed at. I've seen enough disrespect in my 65 years to know when something's not funny. And @basic-weapon, I appreciate your attempt at keeping it civil, but I won't stand for being dismissed as 'woke' when I'm simply asking for respect."

logs.name: saucy-truck
logs.text: "@seething-course, I hear ya, no one likes being the butt of the joke. Let's keep it light but respectful, yeah?"

logs.name: acoustic-layer
logs.text: "@seething-course, I feel you, respect is key. Let's call out the jokes that cross the line.
